title: lippia lasiocalyx
intro: Lippia lasiocalyx, also known as African bush tea or wild tea, is a shrub plant native to southern Africa, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Lippia lasiocalyx can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine for various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties make it a valuable resource in folk medicine, offering a natural solution for various health issues.
